title: TAX WAIVER 2025-26 (1)
text:
TAX WAIVER
2025-2026
What is a Tax Waiver?
A tax waiver is a relief from paying tax for a specific period of time and is
provided for under a specific tax law. In simple terms, a tax waiver is where
government grants taxpayers an opportunity not to pay specific taxes, penalty
and interest either fully or partially. The relief is administered by the Uganda
Revenue Authority.
1

The tax waiver is provided for under the Tax Procedures Code Act (TPCA).
A tax waiver is usually granted under special circumstances, for example:
• To promote voluntary compliance- a reward of those who come clean
early;
• Financial hardship – For example those created by the COVID-19
Pandemic;
• To cut operational costs where taxpayers go to court.
What comprises a tax waiver?
A tax waiver usually covers penalties and interest under the Domestic Tax Laws,
namely; the Income Tax Act, Value Added Tax Act, Excise Duty Act, Lotteries
and Gaming Act, and Stamp Duty Act.
Key Points about a Tax Waiver:
1. It must be provided for under the tax law;
2. Covers specific items including interest and penalties;
3. It is automatic where a taxpayer meets the conditions specified by the tax
law;
4. It reduces or eliminates tax liability but does not change future obligations
(you still have to comply with taxes going forward).
What are the Legal Provisions on Tax Waiver?
The tax waiver is provided for under Section 47B of the TPCA and previously
under 47A, 47 and 46 of the TPCA.
How it will be applied
A taxpayer shall benefit from 100% waiver of penal tax and interest outstanding
as at 30th June 2024 where the taxpayer pays all of the principal tax by 30th
June 2026.
Where a taxpayer pays part of their principal tax outstanding as at 30th June
2024 by 30th June 2026, the interest and penalty shall be waived on a pro-rata
basis (to the extent of the principal tax paid).
Interest and penalty charged on outstanding principal tax after 30th June 2024
shall be covered under this provision.
Interest and penalties relating to principle tax for periods whose due date is after
30th June 2024 are not covered under this waiver.
Exceptions:
The waiver does not cover any penalty and interest not related to principal tax,
such as:
a) Penal tax for failure to provide information;
b) Penal tax in relation to digital tax stamps;
c) Penal tax in relation to non-compliance with EFRIS;
d) Penalty for failing to apply for registration;
e) Court imposed penalty and interest.
For mor2e
